recapPrompt.prompt: To get a range sum from left to right, what do we usually subtract from prefix[right]?

choices.label: The prefix total before left
choices.feedback: Correct. That removes the extra numbers before the range.

choices.label: The current value at right
choices.feedback: Not this one. We subtract the earlier prefix total, not the current value.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain in your own words why precomputing can make later work faster.

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the prefix-sum clues
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the main clue for each kind of prefix-sum job.
hints: Range sums use subtraction between prefix totals.
Pivot index compares left sum and right sum using the whole total.
Subarray Sum Equals K uses prefix sums with a hash map.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You rebuilt the main prefix-sum clues from memory.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck the subtraction idea, the pivot formula, and the hash-map combo.

blanks.label: Main clue for subarray sum equals k
